articles.title: How can HHS drive clinical AI adoption? The industry wish list is starting to take shape
articles.summary: HHS asked for proposals to drive clinical AI adoption. Industry giants Epic, Oracle, Abridge, Aidoc, Tempus, and Doctronic all submitted wish lists seeking federal backing for runtime governance standards.
articles.linkedin_angle: HHS just asked Big Health Tech: 'How do we scale clinical AI?' Their answers reveal a massive blind spot. Everyone wants federal backing, but nobody's talking about the governance crisis happening RIGHT NOW in hospitals. While they wait for Washington, health systems are deploying ungoverned AI. FORGE isn't waiting for permission.

articles.title: AI Governance in Health Care Begins with Understanding
articles.summary: URAC published blueprint for AI governance integration, advocating for building AI oversight into existing quality infrastructure with risk stratification, guardrails, monitoring.
articles.linkedin_angle: URAC just validated what we've been saying: AI governance isn't a new department, it's quality management 2.0. The accreditation body managing $2T+ in health spending gets it. If your health system is treating AI governance as an 'innovation project,' you're already behind.
articles.forge_relevance: Validates Dr. Chen's risk-stratified governance architecture recommendation

articles.title: The AI Medical Services Act: What it gets right, where it falls short
articles.summary: New federal bill creates 'AI Medical Services Provider' (AIMSP) licensure category allowing AI systems operated by licensed entities to deliver clinical services.
articles.linkedin_angle: The AI Medical Services Act just created a new licensure category: AI Medical Services Provider. Translation: AI can now practice medicine (under supervision). This isn't just regulatory evolution — it's healthcare's ChatGPT moment. The question isn't IF this changes everything, it's WHO will be ready when it does.
articles.forge_relevance: Creates new compliance framework for clinical AI deployment

articles.title: Shaping the Future: Navigating State-Level AI Legislation in Healthcare
articles.summary: State legislatures racing ahead of federal AI regulation with focus on anti-discrimination, provider involvement, AI transparency. Legal advice includes inventory all AI systems, classify risk levels.
articles.linkedin_angle: 50 states, 50 different AI governance requirements coming. While Washington debates, state capitals are writing the rules. Ohio says one thing, California says another. Multi-state health systems are about to hit a compliance nightmare. Welcome to the United States of AI Fragmentation.
articles.forge_relevance: Highlights need for unified governance platform across jurisdictions

articles.title: Healthcare AI Trends 2026: Executive vs Operational Reality Gap
articles.summary: Survey reveals 31% of executives claim 'widespread AI adoption' vs. only 3% of operational/regulatory staff. Massive perception gap between leadership and ground-level reality.
articles.linkedin_angle: 31% of healthcare executives say they have 'widespread AI adoption.' Only 3% of their operational teams agree. That's not a communication problem — it's a governance problem. Executives see PowerPoints, operators see ungoverned chaos. Guess who's right?
articles.forge_relevance: Highlights governance gap between strategy and execution

forge_labs_idea.solution: Public scoring system for health system AI governance — like Healthgrades for AI transparency

clinical_validation.source: Dr. Marcus Chen Clinical CMO Synthesis
clinical_validation.quote: Your AI flags septic shock risk at 89% confidence, but the ED resident dismisses it because 'computers don't understand clinical judgment.' Without governance telemetry, we never know this happened. The patient codes 40 minutes later. LUMEN would have logged that override, flagged the bias, and potentially saved a life. That's not just technology — that's patient safety infrastructure.
